m: circumstances; background; how things came about

ex.jp: 事件の経緯を詳しく説明してください。
ex.en: Please explain in detail the circumstances of the incident.

qs.s: なぜこうなったのか、その＿＿＿＿を正直に話してほしい。
qs.en: I want you to honestly explain the background of how this came about.
qs.e: 経緯(いきさつ) = the course of events / how things unfolded over time. 原因(げんいん) = the cause. 理由(りゆう) = a stated reason. 背景(はいけい) = background/context.
経緯(いきさつ) describes the sequence of events that led to a situation (事件の経緯 = the circumstances of the incident). Also read けいい in formal/written contexts.

ex.jp: 警察は犯人を追う手がかりをようやく見つけた。
ex.en: The police finally found a clue to pursue the culprit.

qs.s: 解決の＿＿＿＿をつかむまで、調査を続けるつもりだ。
qs.en: I intend to continue the investigation until I get a clue to the solution.
qs.e: 手がかり(てがかり) = a clue/lead (something to grab onto). 糸口(いとぐち) = a starting point / thread to pull (to unravel a problem). きっかけ = a trigger/opportunity. 証拠(しょうこ) = evidence.

o: clue; lead; something to go on
qs.e: 手がかり is what you use to make progress (犯人の手がかり = a lead on the culprit, 手がかりをつかむ = to get a lead). 糸口 is the point from which you start unraveling a problem.

ex.jp: 政府はこの問題について公式の見解を発表した。
ex.en: The government announced an official view on this issue.

qs.s: 専門家の＿＿＿＿を聞いてから、最終的な判断をしたい。
qs.en: I would like to hear the experts' views before making a final decision.
qs.e: 見解(けんかい) = a formal view or position (公式見解 = official position). 意見(いけん) = opinion (general). 見方(みかた) = a way of seeing. 主張(しゅちょう) = an assertion.
見解 is used in formal or official contexts (見解の相違 = a difference of opinion, 見解を示す = to present a view). It is more formal than 意見.

ex.jp: 締め切りまで余裕があるうちに、早めに取りかかろう。
ex.en: Let's get started early while we still have time to spare before the deadline.

qs.s: 毎日残業が続き、趣味を楽しむ心の＿＿＿＿がなくなってしまった。
qs.en: With overtime continuing every day, I have lost the mental room to enjoy my hobbies.
qs.e: 余裕(よゆう) = margin/leeway/composure (time, money, mental capacity to spare). ゆとり = space/latitude (broader sense of ease). 時間(じかん) = time. 余暇(よか) = leisure time.
余裕 means having more than the minimum required (余裕がある = to have room to spare, 余裕をもって = with composure, 余裕の勝利 = an easy win).

ex.jp: 手間がかかるが、手作りの料理はやはりおいしい。
ex.en: It takes time and effort, but homemade food is still delicious.

qs.s: この書類は記入箇所が多く、かなり＿＿＿＿がかかる。
qs.en: This document has many fields to fill in and takes quite a bit of effort.
qs.e: 手間(てま) = time and effort (手間がかかる = to take effort). 手数(てすう) = trouble/steps (お手数をおかけします = sorry for the trouble). 労力(ろうりょく) = labor/effort (more formal). 時間(じかん) = time.
手間 is the practical effort something requires (手間を省く = to save effort, 手間暇かける = to take great pains over something).

m: estimate; idea; guess; sense of direction

ex.jp: 彼がどこにいるのか見当もつかない。
ex.en: I have no idea where he is.

qs.s: 費用がどのくらいかかるか、大まかな＿＿＿＿もつかない。
qs.en: I don't even have a rough idea of how much the costs will be.
qs.e: 見当(けんとう) = a rough idea/guess (見当がつく = to have an idea, 見当違い = being totally off the mark). 目安(めやす) = a rough guide/benchmark. 見込み(みこみ) = a prospect/expectation. 目標(もくひょう) = a goal.
見当 is an intuitive estimate (見当をつける = to make a rough guess, 見当違い = completely off target/beside the point).

ex.jp: 正しい手順に従って機器を操作してください。
ex.en: Please operate the device following the correct procedure.

qs.s: 作業の＿＿＿＿を間違えると、取り返しのつかないことになる。
qs.en: If you get the order of operations wrong, it can lead to irreversible consequences.
qs.e: 手順(てじゅん) = procedure/steps (the sequence for doing something). 手続き(てつづき) = procedure/formalities (administrative steps). 順序(じゅんじょ) = order/sequence. 工程(こうてい) = a process/stage (manufacturing).
手順 is the step-by-step method for completing a task (手順書 = procedure manual, 手順を踏む = to follow the proper steps).

ex.jp: その記事は多くの読者から大きな反響を呼んだ。
ex.en: That article generated a huge response from many readers.

qs.s: 新商品の発表に対して、予想を超える大きな＿＿＿＿があった。
qs.en: The announcement of the new product generated a bigger response than expected.
qs.e: 反響(はんきょう) = response/repercussion (wide, social ripple). 反応(はんのう) = reaction (immediate, individual). 反論(はんろん) = counterargument. 評判(ひょうばん) = reputation.
反響 is the ripple effect of something on society (大きな反響を呼ぶ = to generate a big response, 反響が広がる = the response spreads). Also means a literal echo.

ex.jp: 光熱費を節約するために、こまめに電気を消している。
ex.en: I turn off lights diligently to save on utility bills.

qs.s: 旅行のために毎月三万円ずつ＿＿＿＿している。
qs.en: I am saving 30,000 yen every month for a trip.
qs.e: 節約(せつやく) = saving/reducing expenditure (not wasting). 貯金(ちょきん) = saving money (accumulating). 蓄え(たくわえ) = savings/reserve (stored up). 倹約(けんやく) = frugality (virtue of not spending).
節約 is about cutting back on use (エネルギー節約 = energy saving, 時間を節約する = to save time). 貯金 is accumulating money in a bank or piggy bank.

ex.jp: この工場では一日に千台の車が製造されている。
ex.en: A thousand cars are manufactured at this factory per day.

qs.s: 海外に＿＿＿＿を建設し、人件費を削減する計画が進んでいる。
qs.en: A plan is advancing to build a factory overseas and reduce labor costs.
qs.e: 工場(こうじょう) = factory/plant (general). 工場団地(こうじょうだんち) = an industrial estate. 製造所(せいぞうしょ) = a manufacturing facility (formal/legal). 拠点(きょてん) = a base of operations.
工場 is the standard word for a factory (工場長 = factory manager, 工場見学 = factory tour, 工場勤務 = factory work).

ex.jp: このアプリの仕組みを理解するのに少し時間がかかった。
ex.en: It took a little time to understand how this app works.

qs.s: 税金の＿＿＿＿を学校でもっと教えるべきだという声がある。
qs.en: There are voices saying the mechanism of taxation should be taught more in schools.
qs.e: 仕組み(しくみ) = mechanism/how something works (explanatory). 制度(せいど) = a system/institution (established). 構造(こうぞう) = structure (physical or analytical). システム = system (loanword, often technical).
仕組み answers the question 'how does it work?' (社会の仕組み = how society works, 仕組みを理解する = to understand the mechanism).

m: to see through; to foresee; to look ahead

ex.jp: 経験豊富な彼女は状況の先を見通す力を持っている。
ex.en: With her wealth of experience, she has the ability to foresee how situations will unfold.

qs.s: 彼は相手の意図を鋭く＿＿＿＿、冷静に対応した。
qs.en: He sharply saw through the other party's intentions and responded calmly.
qs.e: 見通す(みとおす) = to see through / look ahead to the end. 見抜く(みぬく) = to see through / detect (a lie, a weakness). 見透かす(みすかす) = to see through (someone's hidden motives). 看破する(かんぱする) = to see through / expose (formal).

o: to detect; to see through (a lie)
qs.e: 見通す = looking all the way through or ahead (将来を見通す = to foresee the future). 見抜く focuses on detecting what is hidden or false.

m: to be convinced; to assume; to take for granted

ex.jp: 自分が正しいと思い込んでいると、人の意見が聞けなくなる。
ex.en: If you are convinced you are right, you become unable to hear others' opinions.

qs.s: 彼はその噂を事実だと＿＿＿＿んでいたが、実は嘘だった。
qs.en: He was convinced that the rumour was true, but it was actually a lie.
qs.e: 思い込む(おもいこむ) = to be convinced of something (often mistakenly). 信じ込む(しんじこむ) = to fully believe (completely taken in). 決めつける(きめつける) = to judge/conclude without sufficient reason. 思い違い(おもいちがい) = a misconception.

o: to fully believe; to be taken in
to judge without basis; to assume
qs.e: 思い込む = being firmly (and often wrongly) convinced (思い込みが激しい = very prone to assumptions, 思い込みを捨てる = to let go of assumptions).

m: to deny; to negate; to cancel out

ex.jp: 会社はその報道を即座に打ち消す声明を出した。
ex.en: The company immediately issued a statement denying the report.

qs.s: 良い部分も多いが、今回の失敗がそれを＿＿＿＿してしまった。
qs.en: There are many good points too, but this failure has cancelled them out.
qs.e: 打ち消す(うちけす) = to negate/cancel out (a claim, an effect). 否定する(ひていする) = to deny/negate. 相殺する(そうさいする) = to offset/cancel out (financial). 消し去る(けしさる) = to erase/wipe out.

o: to negate; to cancel out; to deny
qs.e: 打ち消す = actively negating something (噂を打ち消す = to deny a rumour, 効果を打ち消す = to cancel out an effect). 打ち消し形 in grammar = the negative form.

m: to cause; to trigger; to bring about

ex.jp: その一言が大きな誤解を引き起こした。
ex.en: That single remark triggered a major misunderstanding.

qs.s: このウイルスは重篤な症状を＿＿＿＿可能性がある。
qs.en: This virus has the potential to cause severe symptoms.
qs.e: 引き起こす(ひきおこす) = to trigger/cause (an undesirable event). もたらす = to bring about (can be positive or negative). 生じさせる(しょうじさせる) = to generate/cause (formal). 招く(まねく) = to invite/cause (often unintended).
引き起こす has a strong nuance of triggering something undesirable (事故を引き起こす = to cause an accident, 問題を引き起こす = to cause problems).

ex.jp: 失敗した時に言い訳をするより、素直に謝る方が信頼される。
ex.en: When you fail, being honest and apologizing is more trusted than making excuses.

qs.s: どんな理由があっても、遅刻の＿＿＿＿は通用しない。
qs.en: No matter what the reason, an excuse for being late doesn't hold.
qs.e: 言い訳(いいわけ) = an excuse (often dismissive: 言い訳にならない = that's no excuse). 弁解(べんかい) = justification/defense (more neutral). 弁明(べんめい) = a formal explanation of one's actions. 釈明(しゃくめい) = an explanation to clear oneself (formal/public).
言い訳 is an excuse you give to avoid blame (言い訳をする = to make excuses, 言い訳がましい = full of excuses). 弁解 is more neutral and can be a legitimate explanation.

ex.jp: 連絡の行き違いで、二人は別の場所で待ち続けた。
ex.en: Due to a communication mix-up, the two kept waiting at different places.

qs.s: どうやらメールの＿＿＿＿があったようで、会議の時間が伝わっていなかった。
qs.en: It seems there was a miscommunication in the emails and the meeting time was not conveyed.
qs.e: 行き違い(ゆきちがい) = a communication mix-up (messages crossing / missing each other). すれ違い(すれちがい) = passing without meeting / emotional disconnect. 誤解(ごかい) = a misunderstanding. 連絡ミス(れんらくミス) = a communication error.
行き違い is specifically when messages or people miss each other (連絡の行き違い = wires got crossed). すれ違い often refers to an emotional or relationship disconnect.

ex.jp: 相手の立場に立って考えることが、円滑なコミュニケーションの鍵だ。
ex.en: Thinking from the other person's standpoint is the key to smooth communication.

qs.s: 上司という＿＿＿＿上、部下の失敗には責任をとらなければならない。
qs.en: Given my position as a superior, I must take responsibility for my subordinates' mistakes.
qs.e: 立場(たちば) = one's position/standpoint (social or argumentative). 地位(ちい) = status/rank. 役職(やくしょく) = one's post/title.
立場 = the position you occupy, which determines your perspective and responsibilities (立場をわきまえる = to know one's place, 立場が違う = to have different standpoints).

m: to switch; to change over; to shift

ex.jp: 気持ちをうまく切り替えて、次の試合に集中しよう。
ex.en: Let's switch our mindset well and focus on the next match.

qs.s: 仕事モードからプライベートモードに素早く＿＿＿＿ことが、健康の秘訣だ。
qs.en: The secret to good health is quickly switching from work mode to private mode.
qs.e: 切り替える(きりかえる) = to switch/change over (modes, channels, thinking). 転換する(てんかんする) = to shift/convert (broader, more formal). 変える(かえる) = to change. 移行する(いこうする) = to transition/migrate.
切り替える is an active, clean switch (チャンネルを切り替える = to switch channels, 気持ちを切り替える = to switch one's mindset). 切り替えが早い = quick to move on.

m: to take the plunge; to decide to act; to cross a line

ex.jp: 長年迷っていたが、ついに独立に踏み切った。
ex.en: I had been hesitating for years, but finally took the plunge and went independent.

qs.s: 大きなリスクを伴う投資に＿＿＿＿のは、勇気が必要だ。
qs.en: It takes courage to take the plunge on an investment that involves a large risk.
qs.e: 踏み切る(ふみきる) = to take the plunge / make a decisive move (after hesitation). 踏み出す(ふみだす) = to take a first step. 決断する(けつだんする) = to make a decision. 踏み込む(ふみこむ) = to step into / delve into (a topic or place).

o: to take the plunge; to make a decisive move
to step into; to delve into
qs.e: 踏み切る implies overcoming hesitation to act (辞表を提出することに踏み切った = took the plunge and submitted the resignation letter). 踏み出す is the first step forward.

m: to determine; to ascertain; to judge accurately

ex.jp: 相手の本音を見極めることが、交渉では重要だ。
ex.en: In negotiations, accurately determining the other party's true intentions is important.

qs.s: 商品の品質を正しく＿＿＿＿目が必要だ。
qs.en: You need an eye to correctly judge the quality of products.
qs.e: 見極める(みきわめる) = to ascertain / judge to the end (thoroughly). 見分ける(みわける) = to distinguish / tell apart. 判断する(はんだんする) = to judge/assess. 評価する(ひょうかする) = to evaluate.
見極める = reaching a definitive conclusion through careful observation (本物かどうか見極める = to determine whether it is genuine, タイミングを見極める = to judge the timing precisely).

m: to hold true; to be established; to come to be

ex.jp: この契約は双方の合意があって初めて成り立つ。
ex.en: This contract only holds when both parties agree.

qs.s: 社会は人々の信頼関係の上に＿＿＿＿いる。
qs.en: Society is established upon relationships of trust among people.
qs.e: 成り立つ(なりたつ) = to hold/be constituted (naturally, as an ongoing state). 成立する(せいりつする) = to be established/concluded (an event: 契約が成立する = the contract is concluded). 築く(きずく) = to build/construct. 支える(ささえる) = to support.

o: to hold; to be constituted; to be founded on
to be established; to be concluded
qs.e: 成り立つ = existing or being valid based on certain conditions (論理が成り立つ = the logic holds, 商売が成り立つ = the business is viable).

m: to happen to see; to come across; to spot

ex.jp: 近所で彼女を見かけたが、声をかけられなかった。
ex.en: I happened to spot her in the neighborhood but couldn't bring myself to call out to her.

qs.s: このあたりでは最近、野生の鹿を＿＿＿＿ことが増えた。
qs.en: In this area, sightings of wild deer have increased recently.
qs.e: 見かける(みかける) = to happen to see/spot (casually, in passing). 目撃する(もくげきする) = to witness (an incident). 見つける(みつける) = to find (after looking). 発見する(はっけんする) = to discover.

o: to happen to see; to spot (casually)
qs.e: 見かける = noticing something without particularly looking for it (見かけない顔 = an unfamiliar face, 最近見かけない = haven't seen around lately).

m: to insist; to assert stubbornly; to maintain

ex.jp: 証拠を見せても、彼は自分は無関係だと言い張った。
ex.en: Even when shown evidence, he stubbornly insisted he had nothing to do with it.

qs.s: 彼女は自分のやり方が正しいと最後まで＿＿＿＿た。
qs.en: She stubbornly maintained to the end that her way was correct.
qs.e: 言い張る(いいはる) = to stubbornly insist (despite opposition). 主張する(しゅちょうする) = to assert/claim (neutral). 言い続ける(いいつづける) = to keep saying. 強調する(きょうちょうする) = to emphasize.
言い張る implies refusing to back down (無実を言い張る = to stubbornly maintain one's innocence). It carries a negative nuance of being unreasonably insistent.

m: to finish; to complete; to put the finishing touches on

ex.jp: 締め切り前夜に、ようやくレポートを仕上げた。
ex.en: On the night before the deadline, I finally finished the report.

qs.s: 三年かけて作った作品を、来月ついに＿＿＿＿予定だ。
qs.en: The work I have spent three years on is scheduled to be finally completed next month.
qs.e: 仕上げる(しあげる) = to finish/complete (transitive: you complete it). 完成させる(かんせいさせる) = to bring to completion (formal). 仕上がる(しあがる) = to be finished (intransitive). まとめる = to put together/compile.
仕上げる = bringing something to its final, polished state (作品を仕上げる = to complete a work, 仕上げに = for the finishing touch).

ex.jp: 環境のために使い捨てプラスチックの使用を減らすべきだ。
ex.en: We should reduce the use of disposable plastics for the environment.

qs.s: このカメラは＿＿＿＿タイプで、現像後に返却する形式だ。
qs.en: This camera is a disposable type, returned after the film is developed.
qs.e: 使い捨て(つかいすて) = disposable/single-use (物理的に捨てる). 一回限り(いっかいかぎり) = one-time only. インスタント = instant (loanword). レンタル = rental.
使い捨て = designed to be used once and discarded (使い捨てカイロ = disposable hand warmer, 使い捨て文化 = throwaway culture). Used figuratively: 使い捨ての労働者 = disposable workers.

ex.jp: メールでのやり取りを通じて、彼女とは良い関係が築けた。
ex.en: Through email exchanges, I was able to build a good relationship with her.

qs.s: 契約の詳細については、弁護士との＿＿＿＿を重ねてから決定する。
qs.en: Regarding the details of the contract, we will decide after repeated exchanges with the lawyer.
qs.e: やり取り(やりとり) = back-and-forth exchange (messages, opinions). 交渉(こうしょう) = negotiation. 話し合い(はなしあい) = discussion/talk. 交流(こうりゅう) = exchange/interaction (broader relationship).
やり取り is the mutual giving and receiving in communication (メールのやり取り = email exchange, 意見のやり取り = exchange of opinions).

m: to accept; to receive; to take in

ex.jp: 異なる意見を受け入れることで、視野が広がる。
ex.en: By accepting different opinions, your perspective expands.

qs.s: 現実をそのまま＿＿＿＿ことが、前に進む第一歩だ。
qs.en: Accepting reality as it is is the first step to moving forward.
qs.e: 受け入れる(うけいれる) = to accept/take in (a person, idea, situation). 受け止める(うけとめる) = to receive/take on board (an impact, news). 認める(みとめる) = to recognize/acknowledge. 容認する(ようにんする) = to tolerate/permit (formal).

o: to receive; to take on board (an impact)
qs.e: 受け入れる = welcoming or accepting something (移民を受け入れる = to accept immigrants, 条件を受け入れる = to accept the terms). 受け止める has more of a 'taking the hit' nuance.

m: to take on; to undertake; to accept responsibility for

ex.jp: 難しい仕事だとわかっていたが、喜んで引き受けた。
ex.en: I knew it was a difficult job, but I willingly took it on.

qs.s: その仕事は私が＿＿＿＿ます。責任をもって対応します。
qs.en: I will take on that job. I will handle it with responsibility.
qs.e: 引き受ける(ひきうける) = to take on/accept (a task, responsibility). 担当する(たんとうする) = to be in charge of. 対応する(たいおうする) = to deal with/respond to. 請け負う(うけおう) = to contract/undertake (commercially).

o: to deal with; to respond to
qs.e: 引き受ける = committing to take responsibility for something (仕事を引き受ける = to take on a job, 保証人を引き受ける = to become a guarantor).

m: to calm down; to settle; to feel at ease

ex.jp: 深呼吸して少し落ち着いてから、話してください。
ex.en: Take a deep breath, calm down a little, then speak.

qs.s: 引越しが終わり、新居での生活がようやく＿＿＿＿いた。
qs.en: The move was over and life in the new home had finally settled down.
qs.e: 落ち着く(おちつく) = to calm/settle down (emotionally or situationally). 安定する(あんていする) = to stabilize. 慣れる(なれる) = to get used to. 落ち着き(おちつき) = composure/calmness (noun).
落ち着く = returning to a calm, settled state (気持ちが落ち着く = feelings settle, 状況が落ち着く = the situation calms down, 落ち着いた色 = subdued/calm colour).

m: to offer; to propose; to report (formally)

ex.jp: 困っている人を見かけたので、手伝いを申し出た。
ex.en: Seeing someone in trouble, I offered to help.

qs.s: 彼はボランティアとして参加することを自ら＿＿＿＿た。
qs.en: He voluntarily offered to participate as a volunteer.
qs.e: 申し出る(もうしでる) = to offer/volunteer proactively (formal, you initiate). 申し込む(もうしこむ) = to apply/register. 提案する(ていあんする) = to propose. 名乗り出る(なのりでる) = to come forward/identify oneself.

o: to come forward; to identify oneself
qs.e: 申し出る = proactively making an offer or proposal to someone of higher standing (辞表を申し出る = to tender a resignation, 援助を申し出る = to offer assistance).

m: to nod; to nod in agreement

ex.jp: 彼は黙ってうなずき、話を聞き続けた。
ex.en: He silently nodded and continued listening.

qs.s: 説明を聞いてようやく納得し、深く＿＿＿＿た。
qs.en: After finally being convinced by the explanation, I nodded deeply.
qs.e: うなずく = to nod (the physical action of lowering the head in agreement). 同意する(どういする) = to agree (verbal/formal). 頷く = alternate spelling of うなずく. 賛成する(さんせいする) = to be in favour of.

o: to nod; to nod in agreement
to be in favour; to support
qs.e: うなずく is the physical gesture of nodding to show understanding or agreement (深くうなずく = to nod deeply, 思わずうなずく = to nod involuntarily in agreement).

m: to visit (sb ill or in difficulty); to strike; to befall

ex.jp: 入院している友人をお見舞いに行った。
ex.en: I went to visit my friend who was in the hospital.

qs.s: 大雪が地域を＿＿＿＿い、交通機関がすべて止まった。
qs.en: Heavy snowfall struck the region and all transportation came to a halt.
qs.e: 見舞う(みまう) = to visit (a sick person) or to befall/strike (a disaster). 襲う(おそう) = to attack/strike. 直撃する(ちょくげきする) = to hit directly. 降り注ぐ(ふりそそぐ) = to pour down.
見舞う has two uses: お見舞い(おみまい)に行く = to pay a visit to someone who is sick/in trouble; and 災害に見舞われる = to be struck by a disaster.

m: to picture; to call to mind; to imagine

ex.jp: 故郷の景色を思い浮かべると、自然と笑顔になれる。
ex.en: When I picture the scenery of my hometown, I naturally find myself smiling.

qs.s: その曲を聴くと、学生時代の思い出が自然と＿＿＿＿れる。
qs.en: When I hear that song, memories of my student days naturally come to mind.
qs.e: 思い浮かべる(おもいうかべる) = to picture/visualize (deliberately). 思い出す(おもいだす) = to recall/remember (a memory comes back). 想起する(そうきする) = to recall/evoke (formal). よみがえる(蘇る) = to revive/come flooding back.

o: to picture; to call to mind (deliberately)
to revive; to come flooding back
qs.e: 思い浮かべる = actively visualizing something in the mind (顔を思い浮かべる = to picture someone's face, 具体例を思い浮かべる = to picture a concrete example).

m: to be a problem; to interfere; to cause inconvenience

ex.jp: もし差し支えなければ、お名前を教えていただけますか？
ex.en: If it is not a problem, could you tell me your name?

qs.s: 明日の会議の時間を変更しても＿＿＿＿ないでしょうか？
qs.en: Would it not cause any inconvenience if we change the time of tomorrow's meeting?
qs.e: 差し支える(さしつかえる) = to cause inconvenience / be a problem. 支障をきたす(ししょうをきたす) = to cause an obstacle/problem. 問題がある(もんだいがある) = to be a problem. 困る(こまる) = to be troubled.

o: to be a problem; to cause inconvenience
to cause an obstacle; to hinder
to be troubled; to be at a loss
qs.e: 差し支える is used politely to ask if something is inconvenient (差し支えなければ = if it is not inconvenient, 差し支えない = no problem / no objection).

ex.jp: 今日は忘れられない出来事があった。
ex.en: Today an unforgettable event occurred.

qs.s: その日の＿＿＿＿は、その後の人生に大きな影響を与えた。
qs.en: The happenings of that day had a great influence on the rest of his life.
qs.e: 出来事(できごと) = an event/happening (neutral, everyday). 事件(じけん) = an incident/case (noteworthy, often negative). 事象(じしょう) = phenomenon/event (scientific/formal). 出来(でき) = result/quality (e.g. 出来がいい = well made).
出来事 is a neutral word for something that happened (日常の出来事 = everyday happenings, 歴史的な出来事 = a historic event).

ex.jp: 採用にあたって年齢は問いませんと求人票に書いてあった。
ex.en: The job listing stated that age would not be a factor in hiring.

qs.s: 彼女は＿＿＿＿のわりに落ち着いていて、年上に見られることが多い。
qs.en: She is calm for her age and is often taken to be older.
qs.e: 年齢(ねんれい) = age (formal noun, used in documents). 年(とし) = age (colloquial: 年のわりに = for one's age). 歳(さい) = age (numerical counter). 年代(ねんだい) = generation/era.
年齢 is the formal term for age (年齢制限 = age limit, 年齢確認 = age verification, 平均年齢 = average age).

ex.jp: 物事をポジティブに考える習慣をつけることが大切だ。
ex.en: It is important to develop the habit of thinking about things positively.

qs.en: Everything has two sides.
qs.e: 物事(ものごと) = things in general / matters (abstract and concrete combined). 物(もの) = a thing (physical). 事柄(ことがら) = a matter/subject (abstract). こと = a thing/fact (abstract).
物事 is a general term for 'things in general' (物事を深く考える = to think deeply about things, 物事の本質 = the essence of things).

ex.jp: 購入前に見本を見せてもらえますか？
ex.en: Could you show me a sample before I purchase?

qs.s: 新製品の＿＿＿＿を配布して、顧客の反応を確かめた。
qs.en: We distributed samples of the new product and confirmed customer reactions.
qs.e: 見本(みほん) = a sample/specimen/model (something representing the real thing). サンプル = sample (loanword). 試供品(しきゅうひん) = a free sample/giveaway. モデル = model (person or object).
見本 is something shown as a representative example (見本市 = trade fair/expo, 見本を見せる = to show a sample, 手本 = a model to copy/follow).

m: putting off; deferring; leaving for later

ex.jp: 重要な課題を後回しにする癖がついてしまっている。
ex.en: I have developed the habit of putting off important tasks.

qs.s: 健康のことを仕事の＿＿＿＿にし続けた結果、体を壊してしまった。
qs.en: As a result of constantly putting health behind work, I broke down my body.
qs.e: 後回し(あとまわし) = pushing something to later / not doing it now. 二の次(にのつぎ) = of secondary importance. 先送り(さきおくり) = postponing/deferring. 棚上げ(たなあげ) = shelving/putting on the back burner (indefinitely).

o: shelving; putting on the back burner
qs.e: 後回しにする = to push something to later (後回しにしてはいけない = must not put off). 先送り is more formal/political. 棚上げ implies the issue is suspended indefinitely.

ex.jp: この機器の取り扱いには十分な注意が必要だ。
ex.en: Sufficient care is required when handling this device.

qs.s: 危険物の＿＿＿＿には資格が必要な場合がある。
qs.en: Handling hazardous materials may require a license.
qs.e: 取り扱い(とりあつかい) = handling/treatment (formal; 取り扱い説明書 = instruction manual). 扱い(あつかい) = treatment/handling (general). 処理(しょり) = processing/dealing with (data, waste). 操作(そうさ) = operation/manipulation (a device).
取り扱い is the formal term for handling or dealing with something (個人情報の取り扱い = handling of personal information, 取り扱い注意 = handle with care).

ex.jp: 自分の欠点を認めて改善しようとすることは、成長につながる。
ex.en: Acknowledging your own shortcomings and trying to improve them leads to growth.

qs.s: この計画の＿＿＿＿は、コストが高すぎる点だ。
qs.en: The shortcoming of this plan is that the costs are too high.
qs.e: 欠点(けってん) = a fault/defect (something that should not be there). 弱点(じゃくてん) = a weak point (already in earlier batches). 短所(たんしょ) = a shortcoming/weakness (opposite of 長所). デメリット = disadvantage (loanword).
欠点 is a flaw or defect (欠点を補う = to make up for one's faults). 短所 pairs with 長所(ちょうしょ) = strong point, in the context of personal pros and cons.

ex.jp: 面接で長所と短所を聞かれるのは定番の質問だ。
ex.en: Being asked about your strengths and weaknesses in an interview is a standard question.

qs.s: 彼の最大の＿＿＿＿は、どんな人とも仲良くなれる社交性だ。
qs.en: His greatest strength is his sociability — he can get along with anyone.
qs.e: 長所(ちょうしょ) = a strong point (personal quality, pairs with 短所). 強み(つよみ) = strength/edge (competitive). メリット = merit/advantage (loanword). 利点(りてん) = advantage/benefit (of a plan/option).
長所 is used in personal context (長所を伸ばす = to develop one's strengths). 強み is more about competitive advantage. 利点 is practical benefit.

ex.jp: 近年、物価の上昇が家計を圧迫している。
ex.en: In recent years, rising prices have been squeezing household budgets.

qs.s: この国は＿＿＿＿が高く、外国人観光客には出費が多い。
qs.en: Prices in this country are high and foreign tourists have many expenses.
qs.e: 物価(ぶっか) = the general price level / cost of living. 価格(かかく) = the price of a specific item. 料金(りょうきん) = a fee/charge (for services). 物値(ものね) = the price of goods (colloquial variant).

o: general price level; cost of living
qs.e: 物価 is a macro-economic term (物価上昇 = price rise/inflation, 物価安定 = price stability). 価格 is the individual price of one thing.

ex.jp: この家具は収納スペースが多く、部屋がすっきり片付く。
ex.en: This furniture has plenty of storage space and keeps the room tidy.

qs.s: 狭い部屋を広く使うために、＿＿＿＿を工夫することが大切だ。
qs.en: To make good use of a small room, it is important to be creative with storage.
qs.e: 収納(しゅうのう) = everyday storage (of household items, clothing). 収蔵(しゅうぞう) = storing a collection (museum/archive). 保管(ほかん) = safekeeping/storage (important items). 格納(かくのう) = storage/housing (planes in a hangar, data).
収納 is the daily-life word for storing things (収納スペース = storage space, 収納家具 = storage furniture, 上手な収納 = clever storage).

ex.jp: 今月は急な出費が重なり、家計が苦しい。
ex.en: Unexpected expenses piled up this month and the household budget is tight.

qs.s: 子育てには何かと＿＿＿＿がかかる。
qs.en: Raising children involves various expenses.
qs.e: 出費(しゅっぴ) = an expense that goes out (急な出費 = an unexpected expense). 費用(ひよう) = cost/expense (of a project). 支出(ししゅつ) = expenditure (accounting/formal). 経費(けいひ) = business expenses.
出費 is money that flows out (出費を抑える = to hold down expenses, 無駄な出費 = wasteful spending). More personal/household than 支出 which is an accounting term.

ex.jp: 大手ゼネコンの下請けとして、建設工事を請け負っている。
ex.en: We take on construction work as a subcontractor for a major general contractor.

qs.s: この製品の一部は海外の＿＿＿＿工場で製造されている。
qs.en: Part of this product is manufactured at overseas subcontract factories.
qs.e: 下請け(したうけ) = a subcontractor (receives work from a prime contractor). 外注(がいちゅう) = outsourcing (to an external party). 委託(いたく) = consignment/entrusting. 提携(ていけい) = partnership/tie-up.
下請け is when a company takes on work passed down from a larger contractor (下請け業者 = subcontractor, 下請け構造 = subcontracting structure). 外注 is outsourcing to an outside company.

ex.jp: 来月末を目処に、プロジェクトを完了させたい。
ex.en: I would like to complete the project with the end of next month as the target.

qs.s: 修理が終わる＿＿＿＿が立たず、代替品を手配することにした。
qs.en: With no clear prospect of when the repair would finish, I decided to arrange a replacement.
qs.e: 目処(めど) = a rough target or schedule (目処がつく = to get a rough idea of timing, 目処が立たない = no prospect in sight). 見通し(みとおし) = outlook. 目安(めやす) = a benchmark. 目標(もくひょう) = a goal.
目処 is a rough timing or completion target (完成の目処が立つ = a completion date is in sight, 目処をつける = to figure out a rough schedule).

ex.jp: この計画にはまだ改善の余地がある。
ex.en: There is still room for improvement in this plan.

qs.s: 彼の主張には疑いの＿＿＿＿がない。
qs.en: There is no room for doubt in his argument.
qs.e: 余地(よち) = scope/room for something (abstract: 余地がない = no room for). 余裕(よゆう) = margin/leeway (time, money, mental capacity). 余剰(よじょう) = surplus/excess. 空き(あき) = an opening/vacancy (physical).
余地 is always abstract scope or margin (改善の余地 = room for improvement, 疑いの余地なく = beyond doubt, 交渉の余地 = room to negotiate).

m: to pile up; to overlap; to coincide

ex.jp: 不運なことが重なり、最悪の月になった。
ex.en: Unfortunate things piled up and it became the worst month.

qs.s: 今週は予定が＿＿＿＿っていて、忙しくて仕方がない。
qs.en: My schedule is piling up this week and I am helplessly busy.
qs.e: 重なる(かさなる) = to pile up / overlap (events, misfortunes coinciding). 詰まる(つまる) = to get packed/blocked. 積み重なる(つみかさなる) = to accumulate layer by layer. 集中する(しゅうちゅうする) = to concentrate/converge.

o: to pile up; to overlap; to coincide
to get packed; to be blocked
qs.e: 重なる = things happening on top of each other (不幸が重なる = misfortunes pile up, 日程が重なる = schedules overlap). Different from 重ねる(かさねる) = to stack/layer (transitive).

m: to dig into; to delve deeper into; to pursue

ex.jp: 表面的な理解で満足せず、テーマをもっと掘り下げることが大切だ。
ex.en: It is important not to be satisfied with superficial understanding but to delve deeper into the theme.

qs.s: 問題の根本原因を＿＿＿＿ために、追加調査が必要だ。
qs.en: Additional investigation is necessary to dig deeper into the root cause of the problem.
qs.e: 掘り下げる(ほりさげる) = to dig deeper/investigate thoroughly. 突き詰める(つきつめる) = to pursue to the very end / think through completely. 探る(さぐる) = to probe/explore. 分析する(ぶんせきする) = to analyze.

o: to dig into; to delve deeper
to pursue to the very end
qs.e: 掘り下げる = going deeper beneath the surface (問題を掘り下げる = to dig into an issue, 内容を掘り下げた議論 = an in-depth discussion).

m: to get through; to be understood; to connect

ex.jp: 英語が通じる国なら、旅行に困らない。
ex.en: If it's a country where English gets through, I won't have trouble traveling.

qs.s: どんなに言葉を尽くしても、気持ちが＿＿＿＿ないことがある。
qs.en: No matter how many words you use, there are times when your feelings don't get through.
qs.e: 通じる(つうじる) = to get through / be understood / be connected (言葉が通じる = language gets through, 電話が通じる = the call goes through). 伝わる(つたわる) = to be conveyed/transmitted. 届く(とどく) = to reach/arrive. 理解される = to be understood.

o: to get through; to be understood
to be conveyed; to be transmitted
qs.e: 通じる = a link or understanding being established (外国語が通じる = foreign language is understood, AはBに通じる = A connects to B, A is related to B).

ex.jp: この地域には大雨による洪水の恐れがある。
ex.en: There is a risk of flooding from heavy rain in this area.

qs.s: このままでは、プロジェクトが失敗する＿＿＿＿がある。
qs.en: As things stand, there is a risk that the project will fail.
qs.e: 恐れ(おそれ) = fear or the risk/possibility of something bad. 危険(きけん) = danger. 可能性(かのうせい) = possibility (neutral). 見込み(みこみ) = expectation/likelihood.
恐れ has two uses: fear (恐れをなす = to be afraid) and formal risk/possibility (崩壊の恐れがある = there is a risk of collapse). The second usage is common in news and formal writing.

ex.jp: 知人から翻訳の依頼を受け、快く引き受けた。
ex.en: I received a translation request from an acquaintance and gladly accepted.

qs.s: 弁護士に事件の調査を＿＿＿＿することにした。
qs.en: I decided to commission a lawyer to investigate the case.
qs.e: 依頼(いらい)する = to request/ask (someone to do something for you). 委託(いたく)する = to entrust/consign (work to another). 要請(ようせい)する = to request (formally, often from authority to authority). 申し込む(もうしこむ) = to apply/register.
依頼 = asking someone to do something on your behalf (依頼人 = client, 依頼書 = letter of request). 委託 is giving something into someone else's care. 要請 is an official call to action.

ex.jp: 余分な出費を避けるため、購入前によく考えるようにしている。
ex.en: I try to think carefully before buying in order to avoid unnecessary expenditure.

qs.s: 作りすぎた料理の＿＿＿＿分は、タッパーに入れて冷凍した。
qs.en: The extra portion of the food I made too much of went into a container and was frozen.
qs.e: 余分(よぶん) = an extra/surplus amount (often unwanted). 余剰(よじょう) = surplus (economic/official term). 余り(あまり) = the remainder/leftover. 余計(よけい) = extra / unnecessary / making things worse.
余分 = more than needed (余分に買う = to buy extra, 余分な説明 = an unnecessary explanation). 余計 is similar but stronger — it actively makes things worse (余計なお世話 = meddling).

m: future prospects; outlook; what lies ahead

ex.jp: 業績不振が続き、会社の先行きが不透明になってきた。
ex.en: With poor business performance continuing, the company's future outlook has become unclear.

qs.s: 不安定な経済状況の中、多くの企業が＿＿＿＿を懸念している。
qs.en: In the unstable economic climate, many companies are concerned about what lies ahead.
qs.e: 先行き(さきゆき) = the near-term outlook/future (of a business or economy). 将来(しょうらい) = the (longer-term) future. 見通し(みとおし) = outlook/prospect. 行方(ゆくえ) = whereabouts / where things are headed.
先行き is used for the near-to-medium-term business or economic outlook (先行き不透明 = uncertain outlook, 先行きを楽観する = to be optimistic about the future).
